Back Submit Conference Topics at a Glance The 2026 agenda delivers the latest technology, strategy and leadership insights across key topic areas, covering the challenges most critical to IT and IAM executives. IAM for Agentic AI Agentic AI introduces new identity, access and trust challenges. Learn how to define, govern and secure AI agent identities, enforce least privilege and apply behavior‑based policies in autonomous, machine‑speed environments. IAM Program and Strategy Successful IAM requires more than tools. Explore how to build and sustain an IAM program with clear governance, roadmaps, metrics and executive alignment — while delivering business value under budget and resource constraints. Machine IAM/Nonhuman Identities Nonhuman identities now outnumber humans. Discover how to manage life cycle, access and governance for machines, workloads, service accounts and APIs — reducing risk from unmanaged and over‑privileged identities. Shaped by both internal and external forces, IAM is now a key capability that businesses need to compete and thrive - from securing the use of AI agents to safely enabling competitive customer experiences. However, its importance is not always recognised, and IAM leaders must work to address this. Developing and leading a resilient IAM function must now be a central tenet of any IAM team's strategy in order to confidently anchor the business from the core. ... With great power comes an even greater need to secure access to the data & services we’re now running with the help of AI. How do you make sure your team gets the right insights without peeking into data they’re not entitled to? We will show how runtime authorization can help and provide attendees with a roadmap to secure the use of AI within the enterprise. ... We will look at traditional approaches and the gaps left across the entire AI lifecycle, grounding the analysis in a case study from a Fortune 500 professional services company that extended its strong authorization framework across its agentic AI enterprise systems. Discuss a practical playbook: a modern, Zero Trust framework for governing the entire AI workflow. ... All 2026 Conference Sessions Gartner Identity & Access Management Summit 2026 dove deep into key topics for IAM around program management, agentic AI, optimizing resources and more. .bgImageParent-1780997312361 { border: none; background: linear-gradient(180deg, #000811 0%, #002856 100%); } Session Takeaways Dive into some of the key takeaways you may have missed from Gartner Identity & Access Management Summit. Day 1 Takeaways Gartner Opening Keynote: Identity at the Core Speakers: Michael Kelley, Sr Director Analyst, Gartner Akif Khan, VP Analyst, Gartner Key takeaways: Implement IAM as the business control plane to enable resilient and secure digital services IAM is essential to modern business, yet its value is often unrecognized due to budget limitations, communication issues, and insufficient decisionmaking authority for IAM teams Change this perception by using outcome-driven metrics, storytelling, and clear strategy communication to advocate for greater recognition and decision rights Session: How to Secure and Govern Microsoft 365 Copilot and Agents at Scale Speaker: Max Goss, Sr Director Analyst, Gartner Key Takeaways: Establish a multidisciplinary approach to M365 Copilot governance, including digital workplace, compliance, identity and security teams. Identify your sensitive information and high-risk users, and leverage data security and identity governance tools to apply protection. Design mandatory responsible AI training to educate employees on how to work safely with AI tools, adopting a “trust but verify” approach. Day 2 Takeaways Session: How is Decentralized Identity Changing IAM? Speaker: Michael Kelley, Sr Director Analyst, Gartner Key takeaways: Explore your environment for any possible use cases for verifiable credentials and determine which of those use cases would make the most sense as a POC. Decide what good looks like for a POC, and if your focus is on saving time and money, or user experience. If the POC is successful, operationalize it and move it to production, if the POC is not successful, fail fast and move onto the next most viable candidate. Session: The Top Predictions of Cybersecurity Speaker: Charlie Winckless, VP Analyst, Gartner Key takeaways: Predict: Through 2030, 1/3 of the work in IT will be spent remediating AI data debt to secure AI. Key Action: Assess data and prioritise remediation efforts for collaborative teams Predict: By 2027, 30% of organizations will require comprehensive sovereignty of their cloud security controls to address continued geopolitical turmoil. Key Action: Inventory your cloud-tethered security solutions and their current sovereignty Predict: By 2030, the C-suite will require CISOs forecast the financial impact of control friction, not just the business impact of cyber threats. Key Action: Design limited innovation zones to allow experimentation with lowered risk Be the first to receive the 2027 conference agenda.
